title: Hoarseness
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Defines dysphonia and hoarseness, describes voice-quality changes, common causes, vocal demand, tobacco exposure, reflux context, and prevention topics.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports dysphonia or hoarseness terminology, voice change, clinician-observed voice quality, duration, and selected history fields.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Defines dysphonia and hoarseness, describes voice-quality changes, common causes, vocal demand, tobacco exposure, reflux context, and prevention topics.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Hoarseness (Dysphonia) (Update) – official fact sheet and key action statements

red_flags.text: Requires history and examination for modifying factors and expedited laryngeal evaluation for neck mass, respiratory distress or stridor, tobacco history, recent procedures, and persistent symptoms.
red_flags.rationale: Supports stridor or breathing, neck mass, persistent hoarseness, tobacco history, assessed neck or airway fields, clinician-led ENT referral, and qualified follow-up.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Requires history and examination for modifying factors and expedited laryngeal evaluation for neck mass, respiratory distress or stridor, tobacco history, recent procedures, and persistent symptoms.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Hoarseness (Dysphonia) (Update) – official fact sheet and key action statements

exact_section.heading: Key action statements: assessment, escalation, and laryngoscopy

treatment_and_management.text: Supports clinician-documented preventive discussion, questions, follow-up, and change in voice or quality of life.
treatment_and_management.rationale: Supports documenting voice care, hydration, smoking review, management discussion, and outcome review without autonomous treatment.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-documented preventive discussion, questions, follow-up, and change in voice or quality of life.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Hoarseness (Dysphonia) (Update) – official fact sheet and key action statements

exact_section.heading: Key action statements: education and outcomes

source_limitations: Throat discomfort, cough, globus, dysphagia, weight loss, general throat or speech examination, combined history, and fixed generic duration presets remain unsupported.
The workflow must not infer laryngeal disease, reflux, cancer, airway compromise, or referral urgency.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
